data.pitchAngle: Your business has no real web presence — your domain resolves to a generic IANA page, meaning customers searching for you online find nothing, costing you leads every day.
data.rationale: The inspection payload reveals that the domain on file for Lees Summit Roof Pros resolves to iana.org (Internet Assigned Numbers Authority), which is a generic internet infrastructure page entirely unrelated to roofing. This strongly indicates the company either has no real website, submitted an incorrect domain, or has a severely misconfigured web presence. Review data is fully stubbed with zero signals available. Because the core business identity cannot be verified from the evidence provided, and both web and review data are effectively absent or invalid, opportunity scoring is unreliable. The pitch angle is valid — a missing or broken web presence is a genuine pain point — but confidence in close probability is low due to thin and unverifiable evidence.

data.pitchAngle: Your website is completely offline right now, meaning every potential customer searching for a roofer in Olathe is landing on a dead page — we can fix that today.
data.rationale: Evidence quality is too thin to make a high-confidence opportunity call. The website fetch failed entirely, yielding zero content signals, and review data was not collected due to a missing API key. While a dead website is a genuine and urgent weakness for a local roofing company, the lack of corroborating signals (reviews, ratings, on-page content) means the full competitive picture is unknown. Opportunity level is LOW not because the lead is uninteresting, but because the evidence base is insufficient to justify HIGH or MEDIUM with confidence. Recommend re-inspection with a live Google Places key and manual verification of the domain before outreach.
